message:
feat(unistd): add setproctitle + setproctitle_init (libbsd) (bd-kp18x)

FreeBSD/libbsd setproctitle(fmt, ...) and setproctitle_init(argc,
argv, envp) — change the visible process name (as shown in ps
output) by overwriting the argv[0] storage that the kernel
exposes to /proc/self/cmdline. Used by sshd, postfix, exim,
supervisord, every long-running daemon to show useful state in ps.

* setproctitle_init walks the argv+envp string area to compute
  the contiguous capacity, then stashes (base_ptr, capacity) in
  a Mutex<Option<ProcTitleStorage>>.
* setproctitle uses the existing render_printf infra to format
  the title, prepends "<progname>: " unless the format starts
  with "-", NUL-pads the entire captured region (so leftover
  bytes from the previous title don't show through), then
  writes the new title (truncated at capacity-1).
* Also calls prctl(PR_SET_NAME) with the first 15 bytes of the
  title to update /proc/self/comm (the kernel-visible thread
  name).

5 abi integration tests using a synthetic argv+envp buffer
(Mutex-serialized): explicit "-prefix" suppression, default
"<progname>: " prefix with deterministic progname, capacity
truncation, NULL argv init no-op, no-init no-op safety. Clippy-
clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
